cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)

storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+/// cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

axon_server::auth_pipeline::JwtIssuer;\n+use axon_server::control_plane::ControlPlaneDb;\n+use axon_server::control_plane_routes::ControlPlaneState;\n+use axon_server::cors_config::CorsStore;\n+use axon_server::gateway::build_router_with_auth;\n+use axon_server::rate_limit::RateLimitConfig;\n+use axon_server::tenant_router::{TenantHandler, TenantRouter};\n+use axon_server::user_roles::UserRoleStore;\n+use axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n+use axon_storage::memory::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n+\n+// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+const ISSUER: &str = \"cutover-issuer\";\n+const TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\n+const DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\n+const COLLECTION: &str = \"items\";\n+const USER_ID: &str = \"u-cutover-01\";\n+const SECRET: &[u8] = b\"cutover-test-secret-32bytes-xxxxx\";\n+\n+fn now_secs() -> u64 {\n+    SystemTime::now()\n+        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n+        .unwrap_or_default()\n+        .as_secs()\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test fixtures ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+/// Build the pre-seeded auth storage (user + tenant member).\n+fn make_auth_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n+    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n+    storage.insert_user(User {\n+        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        display_name: \"Cutover User\".to_string(),\n+        email: None,\n+        created_at_ms: 0,\n+        suspended_at_ms: None,\n+    });\n+    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+/// MemoryStorageAdapter) -> ControlPlaneState {\n+    let db = ControlPlaneDb::open_in_memory().expect(\"in-memory control-plane db should open\");\n+    let db = Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(db));\n+    let boxed: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> = Box::new(auth_storage);\n+    let storage_arc: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>> =\n+        Arc::new(Mutex::new(boxed));\n+    ControlPlaneState::new(db, PathBuf::from(\".\"), UserRoleStore::default(), CorsStore::default())\n+        .with_jwt_issuer(issuer)\n+        .with_storage(storage_arc)\n+}\n+\n+/// Build an `axum_test::TestServer` backed by an in-memory data-plane handler.\n+///\n+/// Returns the test server and the data-plane handler so tests can inspect the\n+/// audit log directly.\n+fn make_server(\n+    issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n+) -> (axum_test::TestServer, TenantHandler) {\n+    let auth_storage = make_auth_storage();\n+    let control_plane = make_control_plane(issuer, auth_storage);\n+\n+    let data_storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n+        Box::new(MemoryStorageAdapter::default());\n+    let handler: TenantHandler =\n+        Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(AxonHandler::new(data_storage)));\n+    let tenant_router = Arc::new(TenantRouter::single(handler.clone()));\n+\n+    let app = build_router_with_auth(\n+        tenant_router,\n+        \"memory\",\n+        None,\n+        AuthContext::no_auth(),\n+        RateLimitConfig::default(),\n+        ActorScopeGuard::default(),\n+        Some(control_plane),\n+        CorsStore::default(),\n+    );\n+\n+    (axum_test::TestServer::new(app), handler)\n+}\n+\n+fn issuer() -> Arc<JwtIssuer> {\n+    Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string()))\n+}\n+\n+fn write_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn read_only_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn make_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n+    let now = now_secs();\n+    let claims = JwtClaims {\n+        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n+        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n+        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n+        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n+        iat: now,\n+        nbf: now,\n+        exp: now + 3600,\n+        grants,\n+    };\n+    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"token issuance should succeed\")\n+}\n+\n+fn entity_path(id: &str) -> String {\n+    format!(\"/tenants/{TENANT}/databases/{DATABASE}/entities/{COLLECTION}/{id}\")\n+}\n+\n+fn entity_body(value: i32) -> Value {\n+    json!({ \"data\": { \"value\": value } })\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 1: No auth header falls through to no-auth mode ─────────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_no_auth_header_falls_through_to_no_auth_201() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    // No Authorization header → goes through no-auth path → 201\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-noauth-01\"))\n+        .json(&entity_body(1))\n+        .await;\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 2: Valid JWT with write grant → 201 ─────────────────────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_valid_jwt_write_grant_creates_entity_201() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let token = make_token(&jw, write_grants());\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-jwt-write-01\"))\n+        .add_header(\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(&format!(\"Bearer {token}\")).unwrap(),\n+        )\n+        .json(&entity_body(42))\n+        .await;\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\n+    let body: Value = resp.json();\n+    assert_eq!(body[\"entity\"][\"id\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"), \"e-jwt-write-01\");\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 3: JWT with read-only grant blocks POST → 403 ───────────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_jwt_read_only_grant_blocks_post_403() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let token = make_token(&jw, read_only_grants());\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-jwt-read-01\"))\n+        .add_header(\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(&format!(\"Bearer {token}\")).unwrap(),\n+        )\n+        .json(&entity_body(1))\n+        .await;\n+    // JWT layer returns 403 because the write op is not in the read-only grants.\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::FORBIDDEN);\n+    let body: Value = resp.json();\n+    assert_eq!(\n+        body[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n+        \"op_not_granted\",\n+    );\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test 4: JWT write → attribution populated in audit entry ─────────────────\n+\n+#[tokio::test]\n+async fn cutover_audit_entry_has_attribution_after_jwt_write() {\n+    let jw = issuer();\n+    let token = make_token(&jw, write_grants());\n+    let (server, handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    // Create the entity via JWT.\n+    let resp = server\n+        .post(&entity_path(\"e-attr-01\"))\n+        .add_header(\n+            axum::http::header::AUTHORIZATION,\n+            axum::http::HeaderValue::from_str(&format!(\"Bearer {token}\")).unwrap(),\n+        )\n+        .json(&entity_body(99))\n+        .await;\n+    resp.assert_status(axum::http::StatusCode::CREATED);\n+\n+    // Query the audit log directly from the data-plane handler.\n+    let audit_resp = handler\n+        .lock()\n+        .await\n+        .query_audit(QueryAuditRequest {\n+            database: None,\n+            collection: None,\n+            collection_ids: vec![],\n+            entity_id: None,\n+            actor: None,\n+            operation: Some(\"entity.create\".to_string()),\n+            since_ns: None,\n+            until_ns: None,\n+            after_id: None,\n+            limit: Some(10),\n+        })\n+ audit_resp\n+        .entries\n+        .iter()\n+        .find(|e| e.entity_id.to_string() == \"e-attr-01\")\n+        .expect(\"entry for e-attr-01 must be present\");\n+\n+    let attribution = entry\n+        .attribution\n+        .as_ref()\n+        .expect(\"attribution must be set on JWT-authenticated write\");\n+\n+    assert_eq!(attribution.user_id, USER_ID, \"user_id must match JWT sub\");\n+    assert_eq!(attribution.tenant_id, TENANT, \"tenant_id must match JWT aud\");\n+    assert_eq!(attribution.auth_method, \"jwt\", \"auth_method must be 'jwt'\");\n+}\n+\n+// ── gRPC composite header parsing ────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+/// Verify that the composite `{tenant}:{database}` header value is parsed\n+/// correctly by `grpc_requested_database` We do this by starting a\n+    // minimal gRPC service and issuing a create_entity call with the header.\n+    use axon_server::service::{AxonServiceImpl, AxonServiceServer};\n+    use axon_server::service::proto;\n+    use proto::axon_service_client::AxonServiceClient;\n+    use tonic::metadata::MetadataValue;\n+\n+    let svc = AxonServiceImpl::new_in_memory();\n+    let listener });\n+\n+    tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n+\n+    let mut client = AxonServiceClient::connect(format!(\"http://{addr}\"))\n+        .await\n+        .unwrap();\n+\n+    // Construct a request with the composite `tenant:database` header.\n+    let mut req = tonic::Request::new(proto::CreateEntityRequest {\n+        collection: \"x-axon-tenant-database\",\n+        MetadataValue::try_from(\"acme:orders\").unwrap(),\n+    );\n+\n+    let resp = client.create_entity(req).await.expect(\"grpc create should succeed\");\n+    let \"grpc-e-01\");\n+}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"0a9f1446-c2e0-4db8-b50e-318c50111461","uuid":"9151c21d-5adf-4d7d-a724-fbf7f18dd1b3","timestamp":"2026-04-16T01:10:02.067Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff data.text: I’m not seeing any existing `LoopError` or `ParkReason` type in the codebase, which means the “surface as Disposition=LoopError + ParkReason=preflight_failed” part is probably a new worker-record shape rather than an existing enum. I’m checking the worker UI and schema next so I can fit the change into the current record model instead of inventing a parallel path.

"""WorkerExecutionResult captures the outcome of one execution"""
type WorkerExecutionResult {
  """Bead that was executed"""
  beadId: String
  """Unique attempt identifier"""
  attemptId: String
  """Worker that ran the execution"""
  workerId: String
  """Harness used"""
  harness: String
  """Tier used"""
  tier: String
  """Provider used"""
  provider: String
  """Model used"""
  model: String
  """Outcome status (e.g. "success", "failure", "timeout")"""
  status: String
  """Human-readable outcome detail"""
  detail: String
  """Correlated agent session ID"""
  sessionId: String
  """Git revision the execution started from"""
  baseRev: String
  """Git revision produced by the execution"""
  resultRev: String
  """Suggested retry delay (e.g. "30s")"""
  retryAfter: String
}

"""WorkerEdge is one edge in a WorkerConnection"""
type WorkerEdge {
  """The worker at this position"""
  node: Worker!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""WorkerConnection is a Relay cursor connection of workers"""
type WorkerConnection {
  """Ordered list of worker edges"""
  edges: [WorkerEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of workers matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

"""WorkerLog holds captured output from a worker process"""
type WorkerLog {
  """Captured standard output"""
  stdout: String!
  """Captured standard error"""
  stderr: String!
}

# ============================================
# Agent Sessions
# ============================================

"""AgentSession represents one agent execution session"""
type AgentSession implements Node {
  """Unique session identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Project this session belongs to"""
  projectId: String!
  """Bead being worked on (null for ad-hoc runs)"""
  beadId: String
  """Worker that produced this session, when known"""
  workerId: String
  """Agent harness used (e.g. "claude", "codex")"""
  harness: String!
  """Provider used"""
  provider: String
  """Model identifier"""
  model: String!
  """Effort level applied"""
  effort: String!
  """Session status (e.g. "running", "completed", "failed")"""

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)
- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)
- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present
- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database
- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path
- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end
- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred
- OIDC federation — follow-up
- Signing key rotation — future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)

Cutover integration tests: JWT middleware + audit attribution + gRPC header\n+//! migration (bead axon-21833a7e).\n+//!\n+//! These tests exercise the full HTTP data-plane router through three lenses:\n+//!\n+//! 1. **Optional JWT layer (ADR-018 cutover)**: requests without an\n+//!    `Authorization` header fall through to the legacy no-auth path; requests\n+//!    WITH a valid JWT are fully verified by `jwt_verify_layer`.\n+//!\n+//! 2. **Audit attribution**: after a JWT-authenticated write, the resulting\n+//!    audit entry has its `attribution` field populated.\n+//!\n+//! 3. **gRPC composite header**: the renamed `x-axon-tenant-database` header\n+//!    carries `{tenant}:{database}` and the gRPC helper extracts only the\n+//!    database component.\n+\n+#![allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]\n+\n+use std::path::PathBuf;\n+use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n+use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n+\n+use serde_json::{json, Value};\n+use tokio::sync::Mutex as TokioMutex;\n+use uuid::Uuid;\n+\n+use axon_api::handler::AxonHandler;\n+use axon_api::request::QueryAuditRequest;\n+use axon_core::auth::{\n+    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n+};\n+use axon_server::actor_scope::ActorScopeGuard;\n+use axon_server::auth::AuthContext;\n+use axon_server::auth_pipeline::JwtIssuer;\n+use axon_server::control_plane::ControlPlaneDb;\n+use axon_server::control_plane_routes::ControlPlaneState;\n+use axon_server::cors_config::CorsStore;\n+use axon_server::gateway::build_router_with_auth;\n+use axon_server::rate_limit::RateLimitConfig;\n+use axon_server::tenant_router::{TenantHandler, TenantRouter};\n+use axon_server::user_roles::UserRoleStore;\n+use axon_storage::adapter::StorageAdapter;\n+use axon_storage::memory::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n+\n+// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+const ISSUER: &str = \"cutover-issuer\";\n+const TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\n+const DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\n+const COLLECTION: &str = \"items\";\n+const USER_ID: &str = \"u-cutover-01\";\n+const SECRET: &[u8] = b\"cutover-test-secret-32bytes-xxxxx\";\n+\n+fn now_secs() -> u64 {\n+    SystemTime::now()\n+        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n+        .unwrap_or_default()\n+        .as_secs()\n+}\n+\n+// ── Test fixtures ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+\n+/// Build the pre-seeded auth storage (user + tenant member).\n+fn make_auth_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n+    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n+    storage.insert_user(User {\n+        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        display_name: \"Cutover User\".to_string(),\n+        email: None,\n+        created_at_ms: 0,\n+        suspended_at_ms: None,\n+    });\n+    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+/// Build a `ControlPlaneState` with jwt_issuer + auth storage so the optional\n+/// JWT verification layer is enabled in the HTTP router.\n+fn make_control_plane(issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>, auth_storage: MemoryStorageAdapter) -> ControlPlaneState {\n+    let db = ControlPlaneDb::open_in_memory().expect(\"in-memory control-plane db should open\");\n+    let db = Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(db));\n+    let boxed: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> = Box::new(auth_storage);\n+    let storage_arc: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>> =\n+        Arc::new(Mutex::new(boxed));\n+    ControlPlaneState::new(db, PathBuf::from(\".\"), UserRoleStore::default(), CorsStore::default())\n+        .with_jwt_issuer(issuer)\n+        .with_storage(storage_arc)\n+}\n+\n+/// Build an `axum_test::TestServer` backed by an in-memory data-plane handler.\n+///\n+/// Returns the test server and the data-plane handler so tests can inspect the\n+/// audit log directly.\n+fn make_server(\n+    issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n+) -> (axum_test::TestServer, TenantHandler) {\n+    let auth_storage = make_auth_storage();\n+    let control_plane = make_control_plane(issuer, auth_storage);\n+\n+    let data_storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n+        Box::new(MemoryStorageAdapter::default());\n+    let handler: TenantHandler =\n+        Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(AxonHandler::new(data_storage)));\n+    let tenant_router = Arc::new(TenantRouter::single(handler.clone()));\n+\n+    let app = build_router_with_auth(\n+        tenant_router,\n+        \"memory\",\n+        None,\n+        AuthContext::no_auth(),\n+        RateLimitConfig::default(),\n+        ActorScopeGuard::default(),\n+        Some(control_plane),\n+        CorsStore::default(),\n+    );\n+\n+    (axum_test::TestServer::new(app), handler)\n+}\n+\n+fn issuer() -> Arc<JwtIssuer> {\n+    Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string()))\n+}\n+\n+fn write_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn read_only_grants() -> Grants {\n+    Grants {\n+        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n+            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n+            ops: -x \"$AXON_BIN\" ]]; then\n+    fail \"Binary not found at $AXON_BIN after build\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Start server ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Starting axon-server on HTTP :${AXON_HTTP_PORT} gRPC :${AXON_GRPC_PORT}…\"\n+AXON_JWT_KEY=\"${AXON_JWT_KEY}\" \\\n+AXON_NO_AUTH=true \\\n+AXON_HTTP_PORT=\"${AXON_HTTP_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_GRPC_PORT=\"${AXON_GRPC_PORT}\" \\\n+AXON_SQLITE_PATH=\":memory:\" \\\n+\"${AXON_BIN}\" serve --storage memory &\n+SERVER_PID=$!\n+\n+cleanup() {\n+    kill \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+    wait \"${SERVER_PID}\" 2>/dev/null || true\n+}\n+trap cleanup EXIT\n+\n+# Wait for the server to be ready.\n+for i in $(seq 1 20); do\n+    if curl -sf \"${BASE_URL}/health\" > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n+        info \"Server ready after ${i} attempts\"\n+        break\n+    fi\n+    sleep 0.3\n+    if [[ $i -eq 20 ]]; then\n+        fail \"Server did not become ready in time\"\n+    fi\n+done\n+\n+# ── Mint a JWT ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n+# We need to produce a HS256 JWT. If python3 is available, use it; otherwise\n+# fall back to the /control/tenants/{id}/credentials endpoint (which requires\n+# a pre-provisioned tenant).\n+#\n+# Here we use a small python3 snippet to keep the script self-contained.\n+if ! command -v python3 &>/dev/null; then\n+    info \"python3 not found — skipping JWT tests (only running no-auth test)\"\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=false\n+else\n+    JWT_AVAILABLE=true\n+    NOW=$(date +%s)\n+    EXP=$((NOW + 3600))\n+\n+    mint_token() {\n+        local grants_json=\"$1\"\n+        python3 - \"$AXON_JWT_KEY\" \"$NOW\" \"$EXP\" \"${TENANT}\" \"u-smoke-01\" \"$grants_json\" <<'PYEOF'\n+import sys, json, hmac, hashlib, base64\n+\n+secret = sys.argv[1].encode()\n+now = int(sys.argv[2])\n+exp = int(sys.argv[3])\n+aud = sys.argv[4]\n+sub = sys.argv[5]\n+grants = json.loads(sys.argv[6])\n+\n+def b64url(data: bytes) -> str:\n+    return base64.urlsafe_b64encode(data).rstrip(b\"=\").decode()\n+\n+header = b64url(json.dumps({\"alg\":\"HS256\",\"typ\":\"JWT\"}).encode())\n+payload = b64url(json.dumps({\n+    \"iss\": \"axon-server\",\n+    \"sub\": sub,\n+    \"aud\": aud,\n+    \"jti\": \"smoke-jti-01\",\n+    \"iat\": now,\n+    \"nbf\": now,\n+    \"exp\": exp,\n+    \"grants\": grants,\n+}).encode())\n+\n+sig_input = f\"{header}.{payload}\".encode()\n+sig = b64url(hmac.new(secret, READ_GRANTS='{\"databases\":[{\"name\":\"smoke\",\"ops\":[\"read\"]}]}'\n+    WRITE_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$WRITE_GRANTS\")\n+    READ_TOKEN=$(mint_token \"$READ_GRANTS\")\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Create the collection (needed before entity writes) ───────────────────────\n+info \"Creating collection ${COLLECTION} in ${TENANT}/${DATABASE}…\"\n+curl -sf -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/collections/${COLLECTION}\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{}' > /dev/null\n+\n+# ── Test 1: No auth header → 201 ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+info \"Test 1: No auth header → legacy no-auth → 201\"\n+STATUS=$(curl -so /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -X POST \\\n+    \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-noauth\" \\\n+    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+    -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":1}}')\n+[[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"No-auth entity create → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+\n+# ── Test 2: Valid JWT write grant → 201 ───────────────────────────────────────\n+if [[ \\\n+        -d '{\"data\":{\"x\":2}}')\n+    [[ \"$STATUS\" == \"201\" ]] && pass \"JWT write → ${STATUS}\" || fail \"Expected 201, got ${STATUS}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 2: skipped (python3 not → 403\"\n+    BODY=$(curl -s -X POST \\\n+        \"${BASE_URL}/tenants/${TENANT}/databases/${DATABASE}/entities/${COLLECTION}/e-smoke-ro\" \\\n+        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n+ ${BODY}\"\n+else\n+    info \"Test 3: skipped (python3 not available)\"\n+fi\n+\n+# ── Test 4: gRPC composite header ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n+if command -v grpcurl &>/dev/null;
